name: Dialog
description: Modal overlay for focused user interactions. Use for confirmations, forms, or content requiring full attention.

modal.description: Whether to render as modal (blocks interaction with rest of page)

whenNot: Simple confirmation that can be inline (use Alert)
guidelines: Keep dialog content focused on a single task
Provide clear primary and secondary actions
Use descriptive title that explains the purpose
Allow dismissal via backdrop click or close button for non-critical dialogs
Trap focus within the dialog for accessibility
Dialog.Trigger and Dialog.Close support asChild for buttons, links, and router link components
accessibility: Automatically traps focus within the dialog
Returns focus to trigger element on close
Uses role="dialog" with proper aria attributes

examples.name: Default
examples.description: Basic dialog with header, body, and footer
examples.code:
Dialog Title A brief description of what this dialog is for.

Dialog content goes here. You can include forms, text, or any other content.

examples.name: Confirmation
examples.code:
<Dialog>
  <Dialog.Trigger asChild>
    <Button variant="danger">Delete Item</Button>
  </Dialog.Trigger>
  <Dialog.Content size="sm">
    <Dialog.Header>
      <Dialog.Title>Delete item?</Dialog.Title>
      <Dialog.Description>
        This action cannot be undone. The item will be permanently removed.
      </Dialog.Description>
    </Dialog.Header>
    <Dialog.Footer>
      <Dialog.Close asChild>
        <Button variant="secondary">Cancel</Button>
      </Dialog.Close>
      <Button variant="danger">Delete</Button>
    </Dialog.Footer>
  </Dialog.Content>
</Dialog>

examples.name: Large
examples.code:
Settings Configure your application preferences.

This dialog has more space for complex forms or content layouts.

examples.name: Link Trigger + No Initial Focus
examples.description: Use asChild with a link trigger and disable automatic initial focus when needed
examples.code: Open via link Linked Trigger Example using asChild with an anchor element and manual focus behavior.

relations.note: Use Popover for non-modal contextual content

propsSummary: modal: boolean - blocks page interaction (default: true)
Dialog.Content initialFocus?: boolean - control auto-focus on open (default: true)
